content: Save-file versioning/migration: store an explicit integer schema version in the save header/envelope. On load, run a chain of small sequential migration steps (v1->v2->v3...) each transforming the raw data to the next shape, until reaching current version, then deserialize into the live model. Patterns: lazy migration (migrate on first read, write back immediately) is cheap and common for game saves since only active saves get touched; bulk migration (migrate all files at startup) is used when every save must be current immediately. Provide sane defaults for new fields so old saves deserializing into a newer schema don't throw. This requires a version field to exist before any migration logic can run (version comes first).
